title: Landing page and public web presence for loopctl.com

story.i_want_to: see a clear landing page that explains what loopctl is, how it works, and how to get started
story.so_that: I can evaluate whether loopctl fits my AI development workflow and begin onboarding

rationale: loopctl is currently a headless API with no public-facing web presence. To offer it as a service, the root domain needs a landing page that communicates the value proposition, links to documentation and API reference, and provides a path to sign up. Without this, visitors to loopctl.com hit a JSON redirect — not a viable first impression for a product.

acceptance_criteria.description: Phoenix LiveView, phoenix_html, esbuild, and tailwind deps are added to mix.exs. The LiveView socket is uncommented in endpoint.ex. A :browser pipeline is added to router.ex with :accepts html, :fetch_session, :fetch_live_flash, :put_root_layout, :protect_from_forgery, :put_secure_browser_headers. API routes under /api/v1/ are unaffected.

Tailwind CSS v4 asset pipeline is configured: assets/css/app.css exists with design system tokens (slate palette, Geist fonts, accent colors from docs/design-system.md), assets/js/app.js initializes LiveView hooks, esbuild and tailwind watchers run in dev. Static files are digested for production releases.

A root layout (root.html.heex) exists with proper HTML skeleton: meta tags (charset, viewport, description, og:title, og:description), Geist and Geist Mono font loading via Google Fonts, CSS and JS asset references, and dark mode as default.

GET / serves an HTML landing page (not a JSON redirect) with: hero section (left-aligned, version badge, tagline, two CTAs), features section (icon grid with 6-8 loopctl capabilities), code example section (curl or MCP tool call), and a final CTA.

Landing page uses Tailwind Plus component patterns adapted to HEEx: hero from hero3 pattern, features from feature2/feature3 patterns, with loopctl design system tokens (slate-*, accent-*) replacing default indigo/gray.

Landing page includes a sticky navigation bar with: loopctl logo (monospace), links to Features (anchor #features), Docs (/docs or external), API Reference (/swaggerui or /api/v1/openapi), GitHub repo, and a Get Started CTA button. Mobile nav collapses behind a hamburger menu.
Landing page includes a footer with: copyright, GitHub link, API status link (/health), and legal placeholder links (terms, privacy).
Endpoint render_errors config includes both JSON and HTML formats: formats: [json: LoopctlWeb.ErrorJSON, html: LoopctlWeb.ErrorHTML]. An ErrorHTML module exists with basic 404/500 pages.
The existing /health endpoint and /api/v1/* routes continue to function identically. No regression in API behavior. WelcomeController.redirect_to_api is replaced by the landing page route.
The page is responsive: readable on mobile (320px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1280px+). CTA buttons have minimum 44px touch targets on mobile. Content maxes at max-w-5xl (1024px).
All text meets WCAG AA contrast ratios (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text) against the slate-950 background. Focus indicators use ring-2 ring-accent-500.

test_cases.name: GET / returns HTML landing page with 200 status
preconditions: conn = build_conn() — uses ConnCase, not DataCase
expected_results: Body contains 'loopctl' in a heading element
Body contains navigation links (Features, Docs, API, GitHub)

test_cases.name: API routes still return JSON and are unaffected by browser pipeline
expected_results: /api/v1/ returns 200 with JSON body containing routes
/health returns 200 with JSON body containing status: ok

test_cases.name: Landing page contains all required sections
expected_results: HTML contains a <nav> element with navigation links
HTML contains an element with id='features' (anchor target)
HTML contains a <footer> element with copyright text
HTML contains a code block with a curl or API example
HTML contains the viewport meta tag with width=device-width

test_cases.name: Root layout includes required meta tags and font loading
steps: conn |> get(~p"/") and inspect the full HTML response
expected_results: HTML contains a <link> to Google Fonts for Geist
HTML contains a <link> or <style> referencing the app CSS

test_cases.name: 404 page returns HTML for browser requests
expected_results: Body contains a user-friendly error message (not raw JSON)

technical_notes: NEW DEPS REQUIRED: {:phoenix_live_view, '~> 1.0'}, {:phoenix_html, '~> 4.2'}, {:esbuild, '~> 0.8'}, {:tailwind, '~> 0.2'} — add to mix.exs deps
Enable LiveView socket in lib/loopctl_web/endpoint.ex — uncomment the socket '/live' block

Add :browser pipeline to router.ex: plug :accepts, ['html']; plug :fetch_session; plug :fetch_live_flash; plug :put_root_layout, html: {LoopctlWeb.Layouts, :root}; plug :protect_from_forgery; plug :put_secure_browser_headers
Create lib/loopctl_web/components/layouts.ex with root/1 and marketing/1 function components
Create lib/loopctl_web/components/layouts/root.html.heex — HTML skeleton with Geist font loading, CSS/JS assets
Create lib/loopctl_web/live/landing_live.ex — static LiveView, no mount/handle_event logic needed
Create assets/css/app.css with Tailwind v4 @theme block defining design system tokens from docs/design-system.md
Create assets/js/app.js — minimal: import phoenix_html, LiveSocket init, topbar
Add esbuild and tailwind watcher configs to config/dev.exs
Add esbuild and tailwind config to config/config.exs (profiles, version, args)
Update endpoint render_errors: formats: [json: LoopctlWeb.ErrorJSON, html: LoopctlWeb.ErrorHTML]
Replace WelcomeController.redirect_to_api route with landing page LiveView route
Reference components: docs/tailwind/hero3.html, feature2.html, feature3.html adapted to HEEx with loopctl design tokens
Design system is defined in docs/design-system.md — Geist fonts, cool slate palette, deep indigo-blue accent, dark mode only
The landing page is fully public — no authentication required
Keep /swaggerui route available in dev/test — consider exposing in prod under /docs/api
